description: 58M, T2DM on metformin, A1C 9.1%, CKD 3a (eGFR 50), albuminuria A2 (ACR 120), heart failure. SGLT2i cardiorenal (R2). Intensification (R4, A1C ≥7%). Statin (R5). GLP-1 RA (R3) — patient has high CVD risk via age ≥55 + hypertension-equivalent risk.

actions.label: Initiate SGLT2 inhibitor for cardiorenal benefit
actions.rationale: ADA-A: Patient has T2DM with heart failure, CKD (eGFR 50), and albuminuria (ACR 120 ≥30). SGLT2i recommended independent of A1C. eGFR 50 is above the 20 threshold for initiation. Empagliflozin or dapagliflozin preferred given DAPA-CKD and EMPA-KIDNEY evidence.

actions.label: Intensify glycemic therapy (A1C 9.1%, well above target)
actions.rationale: ADA-A: A1C 9.1% on metformin alone is significantly above the 7% target. SGLT2i addresses both glycemic and cardiorenal needs. Consider adding GLP-1 RA or insulin if A1C remains elevated after SGLT2i initiation.

actions.label: Initiate moderate-intensity statin therapy
actions.rationale: ADA-A: Diabetic adult age 40-75 should receive at least moderate-intensity statin. Multiple risk factors (CKD, HF) support consideration of high-intensity, but CKD eGFR 50 may warrant caution per KDIGO (moderate preferred in CKD G3-G5). Patient is statin-naive.

contraindications.label: Continue metformin alone without adding SGLT2i
contraindications.rationale: ADA-A: With HF, CKD, and albuminuria, SGLT2i has proven cardiorenal benefit independent of glycemic control. Deferral would miss renal and cardiac protection.
